name: Mobile Conversion
summary: Mobile-specific conversion patterns that account for thumb zones, small screens, and on-the-go usage contexts.

patterns.name: Sticky mobile CTA
patterns.description: A persistent CTA button fixed to the bottom of the screen on mobile. Always visible, always in the thumb zone. Essential for landing pages and product pages.
do: Fix to the bottom of the viewport
Use full-width or near-full-width button
High-contrast, brand-accent color
Show/hide intelligently (appear after scroll past hero CTA)
Include supporting text inline ('Free 14-day trial')
dont: Cover important content (add bottom padding to compensate)
Show immediately on page load (wait for initial scroll)
Use alongside other fixed bottom elements (competing for space)
Animate distractingly on scroll
Block the ability to scroll to the footer
patterns.evidence: Sticky mobile CTAs increase conversion by 10-15% on long scrolling pages. Most effective when they appear after the user has scrolled past the first CTA.

patterns.name: Thumb-friendly checkout
patterns.description: Mobile checkout flows designed for one-handed use. Large inputs, smart keyboard types, and minimal steps.
do: Use Apple Pay, Google Pay, and Shop Pay as primary options
Single-column layout with large touch targets
Appropriate keyboard types (numeric for card, email for email)
Auto-advance between fields where possible
Show order summary in a collapsible section
dont: Require typing credit card numbers when wallet payments are available
Use multi-column layouts for address forms
Show a progress indicator with more than 3 steps
Force account creation before checkout
Open new tabs/windows during checkout
patterns.evidence: Mobile wallet payments convert 2-3x higher than manual card entry. Removing account creation from checkout reduces abandonment by 35%.

patterns.name: Simplified mobile forms
patterns.description: Mobile form optimization: fewer fields, larger inputs, native pickers, and graceful keyboard management.
do: Reduce fields to the absolute minimum
Use native pickers (date, select) instead of custom ones
Set inputmode and autocomplete attributes
Make the submit button visible above the keyboard
Use a single-field-per-screen pattern for critical flows
dont: Use custom dropdowns that fight with mobile scrolling
Show more than 4-5 fields at once on mobile
Place the submit button where the keyboard hides it
Use placeholder-only labels (they disappear on focus)
Require precise input formatting (let the system format)
patterns.evidence: Every additional form field on mobile reduces conversion by 3-5%. Native pickers are 40% faster than custom dropdowns on mobile.

patterns.name: Bottom sheet actions
patterns.description: Replace desktop dropdowns and popovers with bottom sheets on mobile. Native to the platform, in the thumb zone, dismissible by swipe.
do: Use for action menus, filters, and selection lists
Include a drag handle at the top
Support swipe-down to dismiss
Limit actions to 5-7 per sheet
Use icons + labels for quick scanning
dont: Use desktop-style dropdowns on mobile
Show more than 7-8 actions without scrolling
Nest bottom sheets inside bottom sheets
Use bottom sheets for critical confirmations (use a centered dialog)
Remove the backdrop tap-to-dismiss behavior
patterns.evidence: Bottom sheets have 25% higher task completion than dropdown menus on mobile. They align with native platform patterns (iOS Action Sheet, Material Bottom Sheet).

checklist: Is there a sticky CTA on long scrolling mobile pages?
Are wallet payments (Apple Pay, Google Pay) offered prominently?
Are all touch targets at least 44x44px?
Do forms use appropriate input modes and autocomplete?
Are dropdown menus replaced with bottom sheets on mobile?
Is the submit button visible above the virtual keyboard?
Is the checkout flow 3 steps or fewer?
Can core tasks be completed one-handed?
Does the page load in under 3 seconds on mobile?
Are images optimized/lazy-loaded for mobile bandwidth?
Is font size at least 16px to prevent iOS auto-zoom?
